diff:
diff --git a/api/services/sentiment_aggregator.py b/api/services/sentiment_aggregator.py
index c23fea7..a6a50b8 100644
--- a/api/services/sentiment_aggregator.py
+++ b/api/services/sentiment_aggregator.py
@@ -215,11 +215,15 @@ class SentimentAggregator:
             if r.get("spike_detected")
         ]
 
+        bullish_pct = round(bullish / total * 100, 1)
+        bearish_pct = round(bearish / total * 100, 1)
+        neutral_pct = round(100.0 - bullish_pct - bearish_pct, 1)
+
         return {
             "market_score": market_score,
-            "bullish_pct": round(bullish / total * 100, 1),
-            "bearish_pct": round(bearish / total * 100, 1),
-            "neutral_pct": round(neutral / total * 100, 1),
+            "bullish_pct": bullish_pct,
+            "bearish_pct": bearish_pct,
+            "neutral_pct": neutral_pct,
             "top_bullish": top_bullish,
             "top_bearish": top_bearish,
             "spikes": spikes,
summary: Extract bullish_pct and bearish_pct as local variables (rounded independently). Derive neutral_pct as round(100.0 - bullish_pct - bearish_pct, 1) instead of rounding independently. Change confined to compute_market_sentiment(), 4 lines removed, 8 lines added.
risk_notes: bullish+bearish cannot exceed total by design; if they did neutral_pct would go negative — this risk pre-existed the fix
float subtraction epsilon handled by round(); no observed drift in tested range
